_meta.status: PROPOSED / ASSERTED -- director confirms or adjusts. Same claim-status discipline as the tail-lift threshold (CHOSEN dial). Everything here is changeable; the frontier rebuilds deterministically from it.
_meta.claim_status: ASSERTED (not observed). Effect sizes are directionally anchored to UK-energy domain sense (fuel-poverty, cost-to-serve, ToU/flex value, decarbonisation cost) but are illustrative magnitudes, NOT calibrated to company P&L. They exist to ORDER segments by value, not to forecast money.
_meta.what_this_is: A per-household outcome model over the 12 cohort dimensions. Four business outcomes; a weighting fuses them into a single 'business value' whose VARIANCE across the population is the thing a segment must move to earn its place. The WEIGHTING is the core values-call: it says what the company optimises toward.
_meta.why_z_scored: The four outcomes live on different scales (£ margin vs £ churn cost vs tCO2). business_value weights the Z-SCORES of each, so the weighting reads directly as relative importance (sums to 1 in absolute value) and no single £-scale silently dominates.
_meta.anti_goal_seek: R12: these outcomes are DIAGNOSTICS used to rank segments, never targets. No effect size was tuned to make a result look right.
director_levers: business_value_weighting -- THE values-call (a): the outcome mix optimised toward.
effects.* -- the directional model; adjust any level effect and the frontier rebuilds.
params.critical_group_bonus -- how hard the greedy protects a critical group (couples to values-call (b)).

business_value_weighting._comment: VALUES-CALL (a). Absolute values sum to 1.0. Sign is applied in code (margin+, churn-, bad-debt-, carbon+). DEFAULT proposed ASSERTED: margin-led, retention and bad-debt material, carbon a real-but-minority overlay reflecting today's un-priced-externality reality for a UK supplier.

net_zero_tilt.note: decarbonisation weighted as a first-class objective

params.social_cost_of_carbon_note: UK BEIS/DESNZ central non-traded carbon values are ~£80-90/tCO2 in this era; asserted anchor for monetising abatable carbon.
params.reacquisition_cost_note: acquisition/CAC proxy; churn_cost = churn_prob x (CAC + margin_at_risk).
params.critical_group_bonus_note: added to a split's marginal-variance score when it first raises critical-group coverage; encodes 'a segment is justified if it adds variance OR captures a critical group'.

base._note: national baselines for a typical dual-fuel household; effects below are additive deltas per level.

margin_gbp._note: DD-online owner-occupiers cheapest to serve + fattest margin; prepay-adjacent (social+assisted) thin; EV/battery flex adds ToU value; solar reduces volume; price-sensitive erodes tariff.

churn_prob._note: price-sensitive + digital + mobile-tenure churn most; assisted/vulnerable + owner-occupier stickiest.

carbon_abatable_t._note: abatable-carbon = realisable emissions the supplier could help remove. Oil/LPG off-gas heating carries the largest heat-decarbonisation headroom; green-engaged households more likely to act; larger dwellings more fabric headroom.

baddebt_gbp._note: affordability stress concentrates in low-class + social/private rent + off-gas high-bill fuels; assisted-channel proxies vulnerability/PSR. This is where fuel poverty shows up in £.
